nameMeanings.name: Ireby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Irby in Lincolnshire, Cheshire, and North Yorkshire, or Ireby (Cumberland and Lancashire), all named from Old Norse Írabýr ‘settlement of the Irish’. Compare Ireton .

variants.name: Ireton
meanings: English: most probably a habitational name from Kirk Ireton (Derbyshire) or perhaps from Ireton Farm in Weston Underwood (Derbyshire), both named in Anglo-Scandinavian as Íratūn ‘settlement of the Irish’ or more likely ‘settlement of the Irishman’. The reference may be either to a Scandinavian Viking who had been in Ireland or to an Irishman who came to England with the Vikings. Occasionally the name may also derive from either of two places called Irton in North Yorkshire, of which Irton in Seamer parish is a more likely source than the lost Irton in Coxwold. Both the North Yorkshire places have the same etymologies as those above.

variants.name: Erby
meanings: English: variant of   Irby  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Ireby was born about 1307, in Yorkshire, England, her father, John Lord Wake, was 75 and her mother, Mrs. John Wake, was 73. She married Hugh de Moriceby about 1322. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in May 1374, in Kendal, Westmorland, England, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Ireby est née environ 1307, à Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John Lord Wake, avait 75 ans et sa mère, Mrs. John Wake, avait 73 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh de Moriceby environ 1322. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en mai 1374, à Kendal, Westmorland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Ireby um 1307 in Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Lord Wake 75 und ihre Mutter Mrs. John Wake 73. Sie hat um 1322 Hugh de Moriceby geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb im Mai 1374 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Kendal, Westmorland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Ireby è nata il circa 1307 nel Yorkshire, England, sua padre, John Lord Wake, aveva 75 anni e sua madre, Mrs. John Wake, aveva 73 anni. Lei sposato Hugh de Moriceby circa 1322. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel maggio 1374, nel Kendal, Westmorland, England, all'età di 68 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Ireby(이)가 대략 1307년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Lord Wake(은)는 75세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. John Wake(은)는 73세였다. 그녀는 대략 1322년 Hugh de Moriceby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1374년 5월 영국 웨스트모를랜드 켄다에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Ireby nasceu aproximadamente 1307, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Lord Wake, tinha 75 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. John Wake, tinha 73 anos. Ela casou-se com Hugh de Moriceby aproximadamente 1322. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em maio de 1374, em Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Ireby родилась около 1307 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Lord Wake было 75 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. John Wake, 73. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh de Moriceby около 1322 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла май 1374 г. в местности кендаль, вестморланд, Англия в возрасте 68 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Ireby nació aproximadamente 1307, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Lord Wake, tenía 75 años y su mamá, Mrs. John Wake, tenía 73 años. Se casó con Hugh de Moriceby aproximadamente 1322. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en mayo de 1374, en Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 68 años.
